title: Reframe as discrete-time hazard prediction with an auxiliary time-to-pit target
summary: This label is a one-step event hazard, not a generic IID binary class.
rationale: This label is a one-step event hazard, not a generic IID binary class. Survival literature reports hazard-parameterized discrete-time models outperform PMF-style formulations, and recent F1 pit-stop work uses joint lap-level classification/regression heads.
implementation_hint: Build driver-race sequences sorted by lap. Train either a small sequence model or tabular hazard model with outputs for next-lap pit probability plus auxiliary remaining-laps-to-pit regression/classification. Use only past laps within each driver-race; reset state after observed pit stops.
expected_effect: Better alignment to the task structure, especially around pit windows where risk ramps over consecutive laps.
risk: More engineering and higher leakage risk if future laps from the same sequence are accidentally exposed during training or feature generation.
